animations.name: Counter Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Hero stats, pricing page numbers, dashboard KPIs. Animates from 0 to final value to make the scale visceral.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the number is not impressive on its own. Animating a small number (e.g. 12) makes it feel smaller.
animations.reduced_motion: Show final value immediately, no animation.
animations.performance_note: Uses transform + opacity only — no layout thrash. Safe for 60fps.

animations.name: Chart Line Draw
animations.when_to_use: Line charts on dashboard load, earnings plots, hero stat charts where the upward trend should feel earned.
animations.when_not_to_use: For charts that update in real-time — the dasharray animation conflicts with live data updates.
animations.reduced_motion: Set stroke-dashoffset to 0 immediately, show full chart.
animations.performance_note: stroke-dashoffset triggers repaint but not layout. Fine for SVG under 1000 nodes.

animations.name: Progress Bar Fill
animations.when_to_use: File uploads, multi-step form progress, onboarding completion percentage where the end value is known.
animations.when_not_to_use: For indeterminate processes — use the indeterminate variant instead, or users will think it is stuck.
animations.reduced_motion: Set transform: scaleX(finalPercent) immediately, no animation.
animations.performance_note: transform: scaleX is GPU-composited — 60fps safe even at 4K.

animations.name: Percentage Dial
animations.when_to_use: Goal tracking rings, completion percentages, hero stats with a circular motif.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the percentage is below 5% — the arc becomes nearly invisible and the dial reads as empty.
animations.reduced_motion: Set stroke-dashoffset to final value immediately.
animations.performance_note: SVG stroke-dashoffset repaints — fine for one dial, janky with 10+ simultaneous dials.

animations.name: Number Flip
animations.when_to_use: Live scores, stock tickers, time displays where the number changes often and the change itself is the signal.
animations.when_not_to_use: When numbers change rarely — feels gimmicky if used once per session.
animations.reduced_motion: Show new number immediately, no flip.
animations.performance_note: rotateX triggers 3D context — keep flip-digit count under 8 for 60fps.

animations.name: Bar Chart Grow Up
animations.when_to_use: Analytics dashboards, comparison bar charts where bars represent comparable values and the bounce adds liveliness.
animations.when_not_to_use: For stacked bars — the scaleY conflicts with internal segment proportions and breaks the visual stack.
animations.reduced_motion: Set transform: scaleY(1) immediately.
animations.performance_note: transform: scaleY with transform-origin: bottom is GPU-composited — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Donut Chart Sweep
animations.when_to_use: Budget breakdowns, traffic source splits, percentage composition where each segment sweeps in turn.
animations.when_not_to_use: For 7+ segments — the sequential sweep becomes hard to follow and reads as a long wait.
animations.reduced_motion: Set stroke-dashoffset to start-offset immediately.
animations.performance_note: SVG repaints — fine for one donut, avoid animating 5+ simultaneously.

animations.name: Stat Card Count Up + Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Dashboard KPI cards revealed on initial page load, hero stat sections where the card and the number arrive together.
animations.when_not_to_use: In dense tables — the staggered reveal feels slow when there are 10+ cards waiting to animate.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all cards at full opacity with final values immediately.

animations.name: Heatmap Cell Fade
animations.when_to_use: GitHub-style contribution grids, activity calendars, density visualizations where the cascade reveals the pattern.
animations.when_not_to_use: For real-time heatmaps — the staggered reveal conflicts with live data updates and reads as a glitch.
animations.reduced_motion: Set opacity: 1, filter: none, transform: none immediately.
animations.performance_note: filter: brightness triggers repaint — keep grid under 365 cells.

animations.name: Sparkline Draw
animations.when_to_use: Inline metrics in tables, mini trends in cards, compact KPI displays where the line is small but the trend matters.
animations.when_not_to_use: For lines with steep vertical jumps — the drawing reveals the jumps awkwardly and looks glitchy.
animations.reduced_motion: Set stroke-dashoffset to 0 immediately.

animations.name: Table Row Stagger Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Search results, data tables, leaderboard rankings — first 5-10 rows reveal in sequence to draw the eye.
animations.when_not_to_use: When there are 50+ rows — the stagger becomes a multi-second wait. Cap at 8 rows then show the rest instantly.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all rows at full opacity immediately, no transform.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity — 60fps safe. Cap stagger at 8 rows for tables.

animations.name: Gauge Needle Sweep
animations.when_to_use: Speedometers, performance scores, health indicators where slight overshoot reads as 'settling' into place.
animations.when_not_to_use: For precise readings — the overshoot reads as inaccurate or jittery, undermining trust.
animations.reduced_motion: Set transform: rotate(finalAngle) immediately, no transition.

animations.name: Avatar Stack Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Social proof — 'joined by these people', testimonial groups, team pages where each avatar pops in.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the count exceeds 8 — the stack becomes unreadable and the cascade becomes a wait.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all avatars at full opacity and scale 1 immediately.

animations.name: Badge Count Pop
animations.when_to_use: Notification badges, cart count updates, unread message indicators on incremental change.
animations.when_not_to_use: On every page load — feels like a constant nag for unchanged counts. Reserve for changes.
animations.reduced_motion: Show badge at full scale immediately.

animations.name: KPI Delta Arrow Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Dashboard KPI delta indicators (e.g. '+12.4% vs last week') next to the main stat, arriving slightly after.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the delta is flat (0%) — animating zero movement is misleading. Hide the arrow or show 'no change'.
animations.reduced_motion: Show delta at final position immediately, no transform.

animations.name: View Transitions Cross-Fade
animations.when_to_use: SPA route changes, list-to-detail navigations where a simple cross-fade preserves context.
animations.when_not_to_use: For back-button navigations — the fade feels slow for quick back/forward; instant is better.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip the transition; update DOM immediately.

animations.name: Route Slide (Mobile)
animations.when_to_use: Mobile web apps, settings → detail navigations where the new page slides in from the right.
animations.when_not_to_use: On desktop — feels like a phone, not a desktop pattern. Use cross-fade or instant for desktop.
animations.reduced_motion: Show new page immediately, no slide.

animations.name: Modal Zoom-In
animations.when_to_use: Confirmation dialogs, image lightboxes, settings modals where the zoom feels like focusing attention.
animations.when_not_to_use: For full-screen takeovers — use slide-in instead; zoom on full-screen feels disorienting.
animations.reduced_motion: Show modal at scale(1) and opacity 1 immediately.

animations.name: Page Curtain Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Marketing site landings, case study intros where a branded reveal moment sets the tone.
animations.when_not_to_use: On every navigation — the curtain reveal gets tiring fast. Reserve for first-load only.
animations.reduced_motion: Do not show curtain; reveal content immediately.
animations.performance_note: clip-path animation triggers repaint but no layout — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Tab Slide Underline
animations.when_to_use: Tabbed interfaces, segmented controls, settings panels where the underline tracks the active tab.
animations.when_not_to_use: When tabs have very different widths — the slide reveals the math and looks jittery. Use width animation instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Move underline instantly to active tab, no transition.
animations.performance_note: transform + width on absolute element — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Drawer Push
animations.when_to_use: Filters, mobile nav drawers, cart drawers where the main content slides aside to reveal the drawer.
animations.when_not_to_use: On desktop with multi-column layouts — the push breaks grid alignment and feels awkward. Use overlay instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Show drawer immediately at translateX(0); do not push main content.

animations.name: Image Morph Route Transition
animations.when_to_use: Product card → product detail, gallery thumbnail → full image where the shared image morphs between views.
animations.when_not_to_use: When source and target have very different aspect ratios — the morph looks broken. Fall back to cross-fade.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip the morph; navigate instantly.
animations.performance_note: View Transitions API uses GPU compositing — 60fps safe in Chrome 111+.

animations.name: Side Panel Slide
animations.when_to_use: Settings panels, advanced filters, contextual help that slides in from the right as an overlay.
animations.when_not_to_use: For primary content — side panels signal 'secondary'. Use full page navigation for primary flows.
animations.reduced_motion: Show panel immediately at translateX(0), opacity 1.

animations.name: Bottom Sheet Rise
animations.when_to_use: Mobile action menus, filter sheets, attachment pickers where a sheet rises from the bottom edge.
animations.when_not_to_use: On desktop — bottom sheets feel mobile-only. Use a side panel or modal instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Show sheet at translateY(0) and backdrop at opacity 1 immediately.

animations.name: Hero Shared Element Transition
animations.when_to_use: List → detail navigations where a card or avatar is shared between the two views.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the shared element has a different visual treatment on each page (different crop, filter, border) — the morph looks broken.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip the morph; navigate instantly.
animations.performance_note: View Transitions API — GPU-composited in Chrome 111+. Graceful fallback in other browsers.

animations.name: Fade In Up On Scroll
animations.when_to_use: Long-form content, marketing sections, case study paragraphs revealed as they enter the viewport.
animations.when_not_to_use: For above-the-fold content — it should be visible immediately, not wait for scroll-trigger to fire.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all content at full opacity immediately, no transform.

animations.name: Parallax Background
animations.when_to_use: Hero sections, image-heavy marketing pages, storytelling pages where depth adds atmosphere.
animations.when_not_to_use: On mobile — parallax causes jank and battery drain. Also avoid with content users need to read precisely.
animations.reduced_motion: Disable parallax; fix background position with no transform.
animations.performance_note: transform on a GPU layer with will-change — 60fps on desktop. Disable on mobile.

animations.name: Sticky Header Shrink
animations.when_to_use: Blogs, docs, marketing sites with long content where shrinking the header frees reading space.
animations.when_not_to_use: On pages shorter than 2 viewport heights — there is nothing to shrink for; the header never changes.
animations.reduced_motion: Keep header at full size always; do not shrink on scroll.
animations.performance_note: Padding snap is instant (no transition); transform on logo is GPU-composited — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Sticky Stack Cards
animations.when_to_use: Product feature storytelling, case study deep-dives where each card stacks as the user scrolls.
animations.when_not_to_use: For content meant to be scanned — the stacking forces sequential reading and feels slow.
animations.reduced_motion: Show cards in normal scroll flow, no stacking or scale.
animations.performance_note: position: sticky + transform — 60fps safe. Cap at 5 cards to avoid layout complexity.

animations.name: Scroll-Driven Progress Bar
animations.when_to_use: Long articles, docs, onboarding flows where a top progress bar tracks reading position.
animations.when_not_to_use: On short pages — the bar barely moves and reads as decorative noise.
animations.reduced_motion: Hide the progress bar or show it static at 100%.
animations.performance_note: Native scroll-timeline is the cheapest (no JS). rAF fallback is 60fps safe.

animations.name: Horizontal Scroll Pin Section
animations.when_to_use: Storytelling sections, product showcases, image galleries where horizontal movement is the rhythm.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the section has interactive elements — users expect vertical scroll, not horizontal surprise.
animations.reduced_motion: Allow normal vertical scroll through the section, no horizontal pin.

animations.name: Scroll-Linked Image Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Editorial layouts, portfolio grids, image-first storytelling where each image wipes into view.
animations.when_not_to_use: For images with text overlays — the clip reveals the text awkwardly mid-wipe.
animations.reduced_motion: Show image with no clip-path, full opacity and scale 1 immediately.
animations.performance_note: clip-path triggers repaint — fine for one image at a time.

animations.name: Text Marquee Scroll
animations.when_to_use: Logo walls, testimonial tickers, news headlines where continuous motion signals vitality.
animations.when_not_to_use: For text users must read — moving targets are hard to read and accessibility-hostile.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static text, no marquee. Optionally auto-rotate items every 5s.
animations.performance_note: transform: translateX on a GPU layer — 60fps safe. Pause when offscreen via IntersectionObserver.

animations.name: Reveal Mask On Scroll
animations.when_to_use: Editorial headers, manifesto statements, premium product intros where a mask wipe adds drama.
animations.when_not_to_use: For body copy — the mask distracts from reading. Reserve for headlines only.
animations.reduced_motion: Show text with no clip-path, full opacity immediately.
animations.performance_note: clip-path triggers repaint — fine for short text.

animations.name: Scroll-Driven Counter
animations.when_to_use: Stats sections, impact pages, 'by the numbers' sections where the counter triggers on view.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the stat is in a busy section — the counter competes with other motion and loses impact.
animations.reduced_motion: Show final value immediately, no animation.

animations.name: Image Grid Sequential Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Portfolio grids, gallery pages, product image showcases revealed as the grid enters view.
animations.when_not_to_use: For grids with 12+ images — the reveal takes too long. Cap stagger at 6 then show rest instantly.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all images at full opacity immediately.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity — 60fps safe. Cap stagger count at 6.

animations.name: Back-to-Top Button Appear
animations.when_to_use: Long articles, docs, blog posts where the button appears once the user has scrolled past 1 viewport.
animations.when_not_to_use: On pages where the footer is always visible — the button feels redundant.
animations.reduced_motion: Show button permanently or hide entirely; no scale animation.

animations.name: Number Counter On Viewport
animations.when_to_use: Stats below the fold, impact sections, 'by the numbers' mid-page where the counter triggers once.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the same counter is on every page — repetition kills the impact.
animations.reduced_motion: Show final value immediately, no animation.

animations.name: Tilt Card On Scroll Velocity
animations.when_to_use: Premium product showcases, design portfolios where cards tilt slightly based on scroll velocity.
animations.when_not_to_use: On mobile — scroll velocity feedback conflicts with native pull-to-refresh gestures.
animations.reduced_motion: Disable tilt; cards stay flat.
animations.performance_note: transform: rotateX with perspective — 60fps with will-change.

animations.name: Scroll-Driven Background Color Shift
animations.when_to_use: Long-form storytelling, brand sections, color-themed chapter breaks where the page mood shifts.
animations.when_not_to_use: With dark mode — the color shift competes with theme changes and feels chaotic.
animations.reduced_motion: Use a single static background color, no shift.
animations.performance_note: background-color triggers repaint — fine for body element. Avoid animating on large images.

animations.name: Button Press Scale
animations.when_to_use: All clickable buttons — adds tactile feedback that confirms the click registered.
animations.when_not_to_use: On text-only links — the scale feels heavy for a link; use a color or underline change instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip the scale; rely on :active color change.

animations.name: Card Lift On Hover
animations.when_to_use: Product cards, blog post cards, pricing cards where lift signals clickability.
animations.when_not_to_use: On dense card grids — the lift causes neighbors to feel distant and breaks grid rhythm.

code.js:
// Pure CSS — no JS needed.
// The @media (hover: none) rule disables lift on touch devices.

animations.reduced_motion: Skip lift; rely on border or color change for affordance.
animations.performance_note: transform + box-shadow — box-shadow can be expensive on large cards. Use will-change: transform on hover only.

animations.name: Image Zoom On Hover
animations.when_to_use: Product images, gallery thumbnails, blog post hero images where zoom invites a closer look.
animations.when_not_to_use: On images with text overlays — the zoom crops the text awkwardly and breaks reading.

code.js: // Pure CSS — no JS needed.

animations.reduced_motion: Skip zoom; static image.
animations.performance_note: transform: scale on GPU layer — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Link Underline Grow
animations.when_to_use: Inline text links, navigation links, footer links where the underline grows from left to right.
animations.when_not_to_use: On primary CTAs — they should look like buttons, not links. Also avoid on touch-only interfaces.

code.js: // Pure CSS — no JS needed.

animations.reduced_motion: Show underline at full width immediately on hover.

animations.name: Icon Rotate On Hover
animations.when_to_use: Dropdown caret indicators, refresh icons, settings icons where the rotation signals state change.
animations.when_not_to_use: On logos — rotating the logo feels broken. Also avoid on critical UI icons where rotation implies malfunction.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip rotation; rely on color change.

animations.name: Button Ripple
animations.when_to_use: Material-styled buttons, primary CTAs, action buttons where the ripple confirms click location.
animations.when_not_to_use: On text links — the ripple feels heavy. Also avoid on already-busy buttons with multiple effects.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip ripple; rely on :active color change.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity on a small pseudo-element — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Card Glow Border
animations.when_to_use: Feature cards on landing pages, premium plan cards, AI tool cards where the glow signals 'special'.
animations.when_not_to_use: On cards in dense grids — the glow becomes visual noise and cheapens the effect.

code.js: // Pure CSS — no JS needed.

animations.reduced_motion: Skip glow; rely on border color change.
animations.performance_note: box-shadow + masked gradient border — keep card count under 6 per page.

animations.name: Tooltip Fade In
animations.when_to_use: Icon buttons, ambiguous controls, abbreviations where a small hint clarifies meaning.
animations.when_not_to_use: For critical info — tooltips shouldn't be the only way to discover important actions.

code.js: // Pure CSS — no JS needed. Tooltip shows on hover AND keyboard focus.

animations.reduced_motion: Show tooltip immediately at full opacity, no slide.

animations.name: Color Fill Button
animations.when_to_use: Ghost/outline buttons becoming primary on hover, secondary CTAs where the fill signals 'click me'.
animations.when_not_to_use: On buttons that already have a strong fill — the additional fill is invisible and pointless.

code.js: // Pure CSS — no JS needed.

animations.reduced_motion: Instantly fill the button on hover, no animation.

animations.name: Hover Image Reveal (mask)
animations.when_to_use: Navigation items that link to image content, blog category hovers where the preview entices a click.
animations.when_not_to_use: On mobile — there is no hover, the image would never reveal. Use tap-to-preview instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip clip-path animation; show preview on tap (mobile) or always (desktop).
animations.performance_note: clip-path triggers repaint — fine for small images.

animations.name: Nav Item Slide Right
animations.when_to_use: Side navigation, sidebar menus, footer nav where items nudge right on hover to signal interactivity.
animations.when_not_to_use: On top horizontal nav — the slide direction is confusing; users expect down or fade instead.

code.js: // Pure CSS — no JS needed.

animations.reduced_motion: Skip slide; rely on color change.

animations.name: Text Swap On Hover
animations.when_to_use: Nav items, button labels with shortcuts, 'Learn more →' style links where the swap adds dimension.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the swap text is longer than the original — causes layout shift, breaking the hover state.

code.js: // Pure CSS — no JS needed.

animations.reduced_motion: Show only one label, no swap.

animations.name: Avatar Ring Pulse
animations.when_to_use: Live indicators on avatars, online status, recording indicators where a slow pulse signals activity.
animations.when_not_to_use: On every avatar — the pulse becomes visual noise and distracts from content.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static ring, no pulse.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity on a small pseudo-element — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Checkbox Tick Bounce
animations.when_to_use: Form checkboxes, todo lists, settings toggles where the tick adds satisfying confirmation.
animations.when_not_to_use: On bulk-select actions — the staggered tick animation is slow when selecting 10+ items at once.

code.js:
const box = document.querySelector('.check-box');
box.addEventListener('click', () => box.classList.toggle('checked'));

animations.reduced_motion: Show tick at full scale, no animation.

animations.name: Tab Underline Slide
animations.when_to_use: Tabbed interfaces, code tabs, settings sections where the underline slides to the hovered tab as a preview.
animations.when_not_to_use: On tabs with very different widths — the slide reveals the math and looks jittery. Use opacity instead.

code.js:
// Pure CSS — no JS needed.
// .active class is set on click.

animations.reduced_motion: Move underline instantly to hovered tab.

animations.name: Magnetic Icon Hover
animations.when_to_use: Hero CTA icons, social icons, logo badges where the magnetic pull toward the cursor adds personality.
animations.when_not_to_use: On mobile — no hover, no magnetism. Also avoid on form inputs where it interferes with click precision.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip magnetism; icon stays put.
animations.performance_note: transform: translate via rAF-throttled mousemove — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Heart Fill On Like
animations.when_to_use: Like/favorite buttons, social reactions where the heart fill confirms the like with a bounce.
animations.when_not_to_use: On serious content — hearts feel inappropriate for news or finance. Use a star or check instead.

code.js:
<button class="heart-btn" aria-pressed="false">
  <svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
    <path class="heart-stroke" d="M12 21s-7-4.5-9.5-9C1 9 2.5 5 6 5c2 0 3.5 1 4.5 2.5C11.5 6 13 5 15 5c3.5 0 5 4 3.5 7-2.5 4.5-6.5 9-6.5 9z" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
    <path class="heart-fill" d="M12 21s-7-4.5-9.5-9C1 9 2.5 5 6 5c2 0 3.5 1 4.5 2.5C11.5 6 13 5 15 5c3.5 0 5 4 3.5 7-2.5 4.5-6.5 9-6.5 9z" fill="#ef4444"/>
  </svg>
</button>
<script>
  document.querySelector('.heart-btn').addEventListener('click', function() {
    const liked = this.classList.toggle('liked');
    this.setAttribute('aria-pressed', liked);
  });
</script>

animations.reduced_motion: Show filled heart at full scale immediately.

animations.name: Star Rating Fill Sweep
animations.when_to_use: Review forms, product rating inputs, testimonial ratings where the fill sweep adds delight.
animations.when_not_to_use: When users need to click precise values — the sweep makes single-star precision hard to control.
animations.reduced_motion: Show filled stars at full opacity, no sweep.

animations.name: Dropdown Caret Rotate
animations.when_to_use: Select dropdowns, accordion headers, expandable sections where the caret rotation signals open state.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the dropdown is always open — the caret animation is pointless since there is no state change.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip rotation; rely on color change to indicate open state.

animations.name: Button Sheen Sweep
animations.when_to_use: Primary CTAs on landing pages, premium feature highlights where the sheen signals 'polished' and 'worth clicking'.
animations.when_not_to_use: On every button — the sheen becomes tiring and cheapens the design. Reserve for one hero CTA per page.

code.js: // Pure CSS — no JS needed.

animations.reduced_motion: Skip sheen; static button.

animations.name: Skeleton Shimmer
animations.when_to_use: Async content loading, image galleries, dashboard widgets where the shimmer indicates 'loading, not broken'.
animations.when_not_to_use: For load times under 200ms — the skeleton flashes and feels broken. Use a spinner or nothing at all.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static gray placeholder, no shimmer.
animations.performance_note: transform: translateX on a GPU layer — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Indeterminate Progress Bar
animations.when_to_use: Form submissions, file processing, indeterminate server requests where the duration is unknown.
animations.when_not_to_use: When you know the progress — use determinate instead. Indeterminate bars feel slower to users.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static bar at 50%, or replace with text 'Loading...'.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity on a pseudo-element — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Spinner Dots
animations.when_to_use: Inline button loaders, small inline loading states, chat typing indicators where space is tight.
animations.when_not_to_use: For long loads (>5s) — dots feel too small for extended waiting. Use a progress bar instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static dots, no animation. Or replace with text 'Loading...'.

animations.name: Optimistic UI Fade-In
animations.when_to_use: Like buttons, comment additions, list reordering where the optimistic update feels instant.
animations.when_not_to_use: For destructive actions — optimistic UI on delete is risky if the server fails. Use a confirmation modal.
animations.reduced_motion: Show inserted element at full opacity immediately.

animations.name: Spinner Ring
animations.when_to_use: Form submissions, page loads, generic async actions where a simple spinner suffices.
animations.when_not_to_use: When you have progress data — determinate is more reassuring and informative than an endless spin.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static ring; do not spin.
animations.performance_note: transform: rotate — 60fps safe. Use a near-linear curve for seamless infinite loop.

animations.name: Loading Bar With Percentage
animations.when_to_use: Onboarding flows, file uploads with known size, multi-step setup where the percentage builds trust.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the percentage is fake — users notice when it stalls at 99% and trust drops sharply.
animations.reduced_motion: Show bar at 100% with final percentage, no animation.

animations.name: Skeleton Card Grid
animations.when_to_use: Search results, product grids, dashboard widgets where multiple cards load at once.
animations.when_not_to_use: For grids smaller than 4 items — the skeleton flashes too briefly to be useful.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static gray placeholders, no shimmer.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity — 60fps safe. Cap at 12 cards per grid.

animations.name: Pulse Dot Loader
animations.when_to_use: Live status indicators, 'thinking' states, online indicators where a single pulse is enough.
animations.when_not_to_use: For long operations — the dot feels too small to indicate real progress and reads as a stuck state.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static dot, no pulse.

animations.name: Content Placeholder Wave
animations.when_to_use: Multi-section loading states, full-page skeletons where a wave of shimmer ties sections together.
animations.when_not_to_use: For single-element loads — the wave is overkill and reads as a glitch.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static gray placeholders, no wave.

animations.name: Linear Determinate Progress
animations.when_to_use: File downloads, video buffering, progress through known steps where determinate is reassuring.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the actual progress is unknown — use indeterminate instead, or users will think it's stuck.
animations.reduced_motion: Set bar to final percent immediately.

animations.name: Hero Staggered Text Reveal
animations.when_to_use: SaaS landing hero, agency homepage hero, product launch pages where the text choreography sets the tone.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the hero text is more than 4 lines — the stagger feels slow. Use a single block reveal instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all lines at full opacity immediately.

animations.name: Hero Image Clip-Path Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Photography-led heroes, product hero shots, fashion/editorial heroes where the wipe adds drama.
animations.when_not_to_use: For heroes with text overlays — the clip reveals text awkwardly mid-wipe and breaks reading.
animations.reduced_motion: Show image at full size, no clip.
animations.performance_note: clip-path triggers repaint — fine for one hero image.

animations.name: Hero Product UI Unroll
animations.when_to_use: SaaS product heroes, app dashboard previews, tool showcases where the UI unrolls top-to-bottom.
animations.when_not_to_use: For abstract hero illustrations — the unroll reads as a UI glitch on non-UI imagery.
animations.reduced_motion: Show UI at full size, no clip.

animations.name: Hero Gradient Shift
animations.when_to_use: Brand heroes, AI product heroes, ambient brand moments where a slow hue shift signals 'alive'.
animations.when_not_to_use: On text-heavy heroes — the shifting hue distracts from reading the headline.
animations.reduced_motion: Static gradient, no hue shift.
animations.performance_note: filter: hue-rotate triggers repaint — fine for one hero element with will-change.

animations.name: Hero Word-by-Word Reveal
animations.when_to_use: Bold short headlines (3-6 words), launch pages, manifesto statements where each word lands.
animations.when_not_to_use: For long headlines — the per-word stagger becomes a wait. Use a single block reveal instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all words at full opacity immediately.

animations.name: Hero CTA Pulse-In
animations.when_to_use: Hero CTAs, launch page primary actions, signup buttons where the CTA arrives with a satisfying pulse.
animations.when_not_to_use: When there are multiple CTAs — pulsing all of them is chaotic. Pick one primary CTA to pulse.
animations.reduced_motion: Show CTA at full scale and opacity immediately.

animations.name: Hero Background Scale-In
animations.when_to_use: Photography heroes, brand heroes with full-bleed imagery where a slow scale-in adds grandeur.
animations.when_not_to_use: On heroes with text directly on the image — the scale makes text feel unstable while reading.
animations.reduced_motion: Show background at scale(1) immediately.
animations.performance_note: transform: scale on GPU layer — 60fps safe with will-change.

animations.name: Hero Floating Particles
animations.when_to_use: Tech/AI product heroes, brand moments, futuristic SaaS landing pages where ambient particles signal 'alive'.
animations.when_not_to_use: On enterprise B2B — particles feel gimmicky for serious audiences and cheapen the brand.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static particles (no animation), or hide them entirely.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity on 20 small elements — 60fps safe. Cap at 30 particles.

animations.name: Hero Logo Draw
animations.when_to_use: Brand splash pages, agency homepages, portfolio intros where the logo drawing sets the brand tone.
animations.when_not_to_use: On returning visits — the draw animation slows down repeat users who just want to reach content.
animations.reduced_motion: Show logo at full opacity with no stroke-dashoffset.
animations.performance_note: stroke-dashoffset triggers SVG repaint — fine for one logo on first visit only.

animations.name: Hero Trust Badge Cascade
animations.when_to_use: SaaS hero 'trusted by' rows, agency client logos, B2B trust signals where badges cascade in.
animations.when_not_to_use: When there are 8+ badges — the cascade takes too long and feels like a wait before content.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all badges at full opacity immediately.

animations.name: Mobile Menu Slide-In
animations.when_to_use: Mobile site headers, app-style mobile menus where the drawer slides in from the side.
animations.when_not_to_use: On desktop — the slide-in is a mobile pattern; use dropdown or mega menu instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Show menu immediately at translateX(0), opacity 1.

animations.name: Dropdown Fade
animations.when_to_use: Top nav dropdowns, settings menus, contextual menus where a quick fade-in feels light.
animations.when_not_to_use: For complex mega menus — use a more structured reveal with stagger; a single fade feels underwhelming.
animations.reduced_motion: Show menu immediately at full opacity, no transform.

animations.name: Command Palette Scale-In
animations.when_to_use: Cmd+K command palettes, search modals, quick-action panels where a fast scale-in feels snappy.
animations.when_not_to_use: On mobile — command palettes don't fit mobile UX well; use a bottom sheet instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Show palette immediately at scale(1), opacity 1.

animations.name: Accordion Expand
animations.when_to_use: FAQ sections, settings sections, sidebar menus, docs navigation where content expands smoothly.
animations.when_not_to_use: For single-line content — the expand feels like overkill; just toggle visibility instantly.
animations.reduced_motion: Show panel content immediately, no expand animation.
animations.performance_note: grid-template-rows is GPU-friendly in modern browsers (Chrome 117+, Firefox 119+). Fallback uses max-height.

animations.name: Mega Menu Reveal
animations.when_to_use: E-commerce category navigation, docs site navigation, enterprise SaaS top nav with many items.
animations.when_not_to_use: For sites with fewer than 6 top-level items — mega menu is overkill; use a simple dropdown.

code.js:
// Pure CSS — no JS needed. Reveals on hover AND keyboard focus.
// Add @media (hover: none) for mobile fallback that uses tap.

animations.reduced_motion: Show mega menu immediately at full opacity, no clip.

animations.name: Tab Switch Slide
animations.when_to_use: Settings panels, dashboard tabs, multi-step forms where the content slides between tabs.
animations.when_not_to_use: For tabs with form inputs — sliding loses focus state and confuses screen readers.
animations.reduced_motion: Show new panel immediately at full opacity, no slide.

animations.name: Breadcrumb Trickle
animations.when_to_use: Deep docs hierarchies, e-commerce category pages, file explorers where the path reveals in sequence.
animations.when_not_to_use: On simple 2-level hierarchies — the trickle is overkill; just show the breadcrumb statically.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all crumbs at full opacity immediately.

animations.name: Sidebar Collapse
animations.when_to_use: Dashboards, IDEs, multi-panel apps where the sidebar collapses to icon-only mode.
animations.when_not_to_use: On mobile — sidebars should be drawers, not collapsible columns; mobile has no room for both.
animations.reduced_motion: Snap sidebar to collapsed state immediately, no animation.

animations.name: Toast Slide-In
animations.when_to_use: Action confirmations, error notifications, save status where the toast slides in from the corner.
animations.when_not_to_use: For critical errors — use a modal that requires acknowledgment; toasts can be missed.
animations.reduced_motion: Show toast immediately at full opacity, no slide.

animations.name: Notification Stack
animations.when_to_use: In-app notification centers, activity feeds, chat message groups where items cascade in.
animations.when_not_to_use: For more than 5 notifications at once — the stack becomes overwhelming and unreadable.
animations.reduced_motion: Show all notifications at full opacity immediately, no cascade.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity — 60fps safe. Cap at 5 simultaneous notifications.

animations.name: Drag Card Reorder
animations.when_to_use: Kanban boards, task lists, sortable galleries where users drag to reorder.
animations.when_not_to_use: For lists with 50+ items — drag performance degrades; use virtualization or pagination.
animations.reduced_motion: Allow reordering via up/down buttons or click-to-move; no drag.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity — 60fps safe. Use HTML5 DnD or pointer events; avoid frameworks for simple cases.

animations.name: Swipe To Dismiss
animations.when_to_use: Notification cards, mobile inbox items, story cards where swipe reveals dismiss action.
animations.when_not_to_use: For desktop — swipe gestures aren't native to mouse interaction; use an explicit dismiss button.
animations.reduced_motion: Show dismiss button on card; no swipe gesture.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity via touch events — 60fps safe on mobile.

animations.name: Pull-To-Refresh
animations.when_to_use: Mobile feed apps, mobile message lists, mobile dashboards where pull-down triggers refresh.
animations.when_not_to_use: On desktop — pull-to-refresh isn't a desktop pattern; use a refresh button instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Show refresh button at top of feed; no pull gesture.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity via touch events — 60fps safe on mobile.

animations.name: Magnetic Button
animations.when_to_use: Hero CTAs, premium product CTAs, agency homepage buttons where the magnetic pull adds delight.
animations.when_not_to_use: On forms — magnetic buttons interfere with click precision and accessibility.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip magnetism; button stays put.
animations.performance_note: transform: translate via mousemove with rAF throttle — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Tilt Card (3D)
animations.when_to_use: Premium product cards, feature highlights, app preview cards where 3D tilt adds depth.
animations.when_not_to_use: On mobile — no hover, no tilt. Also avoid on cards with form inputs; tilt breaks input precision.
animations.reduced_motion: Skip tilt; card stays flat.
animations.performance_note: transform: rotateX/rotateY with perspective — 60fps safe with will-change.

animations.name: Long-Press Feedback
animations.when_to_use: Mobile long-press menus, reorder handles, quick actions where a growing ring indicates press progress.
animations.when_not_to_use: On desktop — long-press isn't a desktop pattern; use right-click context menu instead.
animations.reduced_motion: Use an explicit context menu button instead of long-press.
animations.performance_note: transform + opacity via touch events — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Slider Snap
animations.when_to_use: Image carousels, value sliders, segmented controls where snap-to-value feels precise.
animations.when_not_to_use: For continuous inputs — snapping breaks the smooth feel and feels restrictive.
animations.reduced_motion: Move thumb instantly to snapped position, no animation.

animations.name: Pinch Zoom Image
animations.when_to_use: Image lightboxes, map views, product image galleries where pinch zooms into detail.
animations.when_not_to_use: For text content — pinch-zoom on text is confusing and conflicts with browser zoom.
animations.reduced_motion: Use explicit zoom buttons (+/-) instead of pinch gesture.
animations.performance_note: transform: scale via touch events — 60fps safe.

animations.name: Drop Zone Highlight
animations.when_to_use: File upload zones, drag-drop interfaces, kanban card drops where the highlight signals 'drop here'.
animations.when_not_to_use: When the drop zone is small — the highlight competes with the content and obscures the target.
animations.reduced_motion: Show static highlighted border on drop zone; no animation.

animations.name: Scroll-To-Zoom Map
animations.when_to_use: Interactive maps, image zoom interfaces, floor plans where ctrl+scroll zooms in and out.
animations.when_not_to_use: On regular scroll pages — ctrl+scroll conflicts with browser zoom and confuses users.
animations.reduced_motion: Use explicit zoom buttons (+/-) instead of ctrl+scroll.
animations.performance_note: transform: scale via wheel events — 60fps safe with rAF throttle.
